name: ◁ BRAISED BEANS IN COCONUT MILK WITH SHIITAKE MUSHROOMS

ingredients.item:
My mom makes this dish (đậu ngự rim dừa và nấm hương) quite often during the spring and summer, when fresh lima beans are in season. If you can’t find fresh lima beans in your location, the dried kind will work as well. Just rehydrate them by soaking them in water overnight.
SKILL LEVELModerate|YIELD4 servings
3½ ounces (100 g) fresh (or dried, soaked for 8 to 10 hours and drained) lima beans
3½ ounces (100 g) fresh (or dried, soaked for 8 to 10 hours and drained) pinto beans
Pinch of salt
2 tablespoons vegetable oil
1 tablespoon minced leek (white part only)
ingredients.original:
My mom makes this dish (đậu ngự rim dừa và nấm hương) quite often during the spring and summer, when fresh lima beans are in season. If you can’t find fresh lima beans in your location, the dried kind will work as well. Just rehydrate them by soaking them in water overnight.
SKILL LEVELModerate|YIELD4 servings
3½ ounces (100 g) fresh (or dried, soaked for 8 to 10 hours and drained) lima beans
3½ ounces (100 g) fresh (or dried, soaked for 8 to 10 hours and drained) pinto beans
Pinch of salt
2 tablespoons vegetable oil
1 tablespoon minced leek (white part only)

ingredients.item: ½ ounce (14 g) dried shiitake mushrooms, soaked and large caps halved and small caps quartered
ingredients.original: ½ ounce (14 g) dried shiitake mushrooms, soaked and large caps halved and small caps quartered

ingredients.item: 3½ ounces (100 g) mature coconut meat (see Note), cut into thin, small slices
ingredients.original: 3½ ounces (100 g) mature coconut meat (see Note), cut into thin, small slices

ingredients.item: 5½ ounces (165 ml) full-fat, unsweetened coconut milk
ingredients.original: 5½ ounces (165 ml) full-fat, unsweetened coconut milk

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon vegetable or mushroom stock powder
ingredients.original: 1 teaspoon vegetable or mushroom stock powder

ingredients.item: 1 cube store-bought or homemade chao, mashed
ingredients.original: 1 cube store-bought or homemade chao, mashed

ingredients.item: 1. Fill a large saucepan halfway with water and bring to a boil over high heat. Add beans and salt, reduce the heat to medium, and cook until softened (about 15 minutes). Drain and transfer to a bowl.
ingredients.original: 1. Fill a large saucepan halfway with water and bring to a boil over high heat. Add beans and salt, reduce the heat to medium, and cook until softened (about 15 minutes). Drain and transfer to a bowl.

ingredients.item: 2. In a medium skillet, heat vegetable oil over low heat. When hot, add leek and cook and stir until slightly golden around the edges (about 30 seconds). Add mushrooms and soy sauce and stir well. Add cooked beans, coconut meat and milk, and stock powder, and continue to cook, stirring often, until bubbly (about 3 minutes).
ingredients.original: 2. In a medium skillet, heat vegetable oil over low heat. When hot, add leek and cook and stir until slightly golden around the edges (about 30 seconds). Add mushrooms and soy sauce and stir well. Add cooked beans, coconut meat and milk, and stock powder, and continue to cook, stirring often, until bubbly (about 3 minutes).

ingredients.item: 3. Stir in ginger and chao and continue to cook on low heat, stirring often, until slightly thickened (5 to 10 minutes). Remove from heat.
ingredients.original: 3. Stir in ginger and chao and continue to cook on low heat, stirring often, until slightly thickened (5 to 10 minutes). Remove from heat.

ingredients.item: 4. Transfer to serving bowls and sprinkle with black sesame seeds.
ingredients.original: 4. Transfer to serving bowls and sprinkle with black sesame seeds.
